commands.review_summary:
The PR changes channel schema metadata collection so same-origin replacement channel manifests keep or take channel schema ownership via `preferOver`, and adds Slack validation regression coverage for both registry orders.

PR surface: Source +43, Tests +86. Total +129 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Partly: the related issue has a redacted fixture and current source shows same-origin schema ownership can depend on registry order, but I did not run a live current-main replacement-bundle repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the remaining blockers are contributor real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of the compatibility-sensitive precedence rule, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(config): honor replacement channel config schemas
target.body:
Fixes #92884

## Summary
- make channel schema metadata deterministic for same-origin replacement channel plugins
- allow a channel config schema that declares `preferOver` to keep ownership of the active channel schema instead of being overwritten by later bundled metadata
- add Slack replacement regression coverage with both registry orders so `channels.slack.threadGuard` validates consistently

## Tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/validation.channel-metadata.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/validation.channel-metadata.test.ts src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts src/cli/config-cli.test.ts src/config/runtime-schema.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
target.author: yonganzhang
